title: CPU and memory usage of Podman Desktop app after update from 5.2.5 to 5.3.1 on Windows
body:
### Issue Description

All details are here: https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop/issues/10356. 

I thought it was a problem with the desktop app, but as I reverted to podman `5.2.5` from `5.3.1`, the problem disappeared. In the other thread I've also dumped some Process Manager inspection of the podman desktop app. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Update podman to 5.3.1 (possibly less) from 5.2.5
2. Start Podma Destkop (both 1.13.3 and 1.15 have the problem)
3. Logs about failed connections start to pop, the app builds up memory and increasing CPU pressure, becomes unresponsive.


### Describe the results you received

`Podman-desktop` clogs the system with memory and cpu, increasing in time.

### Describe the results you expected

`podman-desktop` app keeps working as expected.

### Additional information

comments.body:
This is a podman-desktop issue, not a podman issue. sadly since podman-desktop moved we can no longer just shift the issue over to them.

Please open this issue under https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop/issues

@rhatdan Thanks, I had already created the issue there. 

Are you sure tho it's a podman-desktop issue? Because I have the problem only after a `podman` update tho.
It might be a difference between podman-desktop version not communicating well with the podman in the podman machine, but it still would need to be diagnosed there.
